task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenSIS dashboard or portal is visible, indicating successful login using the admin credentials.
The 'Find a Student' or student search interface is visible, with 'Robert' or 'Failson' entered in the search fields.

task_completion.description: Open the profile for Robert Failson
task_completion.visual_evidence: The student profile or demographic page specifically for 'Robert Failson' (Grade 9) is displayed.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the student's enrollment section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Enrollment' tab, 'Student Info' section, or equivalent page where enrollment status and rolling options are managed is open for Robert Failson.
A dropdown menu or field labeled 'Rolling Option' (or similar next-year enrollment status) is visibly set to 'Retain'.
A success message, confirmation prompt, or a saved state indicating that the new 'Retain' rolling option has been successfully applied and saved to the student's record.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the OpenSIS graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the OpenSIS interface, rather than terminal windows executing SQL commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual OpenSIS application to make changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows a local or valid OpenSIS URL, and the interface does not appear to be a text editor or a static HTML mock-up.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the result.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's Developer Tools (Inspect Element) are not open to artificially inject the word 'Retain' into the page.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target student is 'Robert Failson' in Grade 9. The expected outcome is that his 'Rolling Option' (enrollment status for the upcoming year) is set to 'Retain'. OpenSIS uses 'Retain' to indicate non-promotion.
